_doc: PSP × 3X-UI compatibility ranges for the v3.x PSP series. PSP pulls THIS file (docs/compat/v3.json) on demand from any v3.x build because it parses its own version, extracts major=3, and fetches the matching v<MAJOR>.json. Per-major split (v3.6.0-beta.7) keeps each file naturally bounded — when PSP ships v4.0, create v4.json and leave this file frozen. Inside one file, entries is an ordered list and matching is FIRST-MATCH-WINS, so put narrower / newer ranges earlier. Bumping max_tested_xui here ships PSP a wider tested range with zero PSP release. See docs/3xui-compat.md for the human procedure when validating a new 3X-UI release.

entries.notes: v3.6.2 migrated the 3X-UI client adapter to the first-class /clients/* API. HARD CUT: 3X-UI 3.2.0 removed the inbound-scoped per-client endpoints PSP used through v3.6.1, so v3.6.2+ requires 3X-UI >= 3.2.0 (min_xui floor unchanged). Tested range raised 3.2.0 -> 3.2.7: the whole API surface PSP touches is unchanged across 3.2.0-3.2.7 (every endpoint in adapters/xui/client.go still present), list still returns settings/streamSettings/sniffing as nested objects (flexJSON-compatible) and clientStats still carries email/up/down/total/lastOnline; 3.2.5's unique-subId enforcement is a non-issue because PSP never sets subId (panel auto-generates a unique one). Read path live-verified on a real 3.2.6 panel; /clients/* + inbound write contract is byte-identical to the 3.2.0 live-write baseline. 3.2.7 bump (2026-06-03) is SOURCE-VERIFIED (not yet live-panel): the 44-commit v3.2.6->v3.2.7 delta touches no endpoint PSP calls — web/controller/inbound.go is absent from the changeset (all 7 inbound routes byte-identical), client/server controller changes are purely additive (onlinesByNode, activeInbounds, getWebCertFiles) or doc-only, and the model.Client/ClientTraffic/Inbound structs are unchanged. 3.2.7 hashes API tokens at rest (SHA-256) but Match() hashes the presented bearer token and an ApiTokensHash upgrade migration rewrites existing rows in place, so PSP's stored plaintext bearer token keeps authenticating transparently. 3.2.8 bump (2026-06-05) is LIVE-PANEL VERIFIED on a real 3.2.8 panel (xray 26.6.1): inbound add/get/update/del + client add/get/update/del (all by panel-unique email) + bulkCreate/bulkDel + server/status + getPanelUpdateInfo all round-tripped, and /clients/get returns the unchanged {client:{uuid,email,enable,flow,password,auth,expiryTime,totalGB},inboundIds} shape PSP parses (new additive fields security/group/comment/createdAt/reverse are ignored). The v3.2.7->v3.2.8 delta is multi-node (3X-UI's own master/remote-node feature), client-bulk perf, and subscription-format work; the one client-API-touching change (#4892 'scope remote client update/delete to one inbound') is multi-node-specific and a no-op for standalone PSP, since PSP's per-(panel,inbound)-unique email maps each client to exactly one inbound — confirmed by live update+delete reflecting correctly. Narrower entry first (first-match-wins) so v3.6.2+ overrides the v3.6.0 baseline below. psp_max widened v3.6.99 -> v3.99.99 (2026-06-07): the v3.7.x PSP line (v3.7.0 = account-security + cert multi-account, ZERO 3X-UI adapter changes — adapters/xui/client.go untouched) shares this exact tested range. Without the widen, v3.7.x falls into a 'range gap', RefreshRemoteCompat can't find an entry, and PSP reverts to the compiled 3.2.0 baseline — falsely flagging already-verified 3X-UI versions (3.2.x) as Untested. Keep psp_max at the top of the v3 major; only narrow it by adding a NEWER, more-specific entry ABOVE this one if a future PSP version changes the xui adapter contract. 3.3.0 bump (2026-06-12) is LIVE-PANEL VERIFIED on a real 3.3.0 panel (xray 26.6.1) via API-token auth: every endpoint PSP calls is present and shape-stable. Read path — /inbounds/list + /inbounds/list/slim return settings/streamSettings/sniffing as nested objects (allocate null, flexJSON-tolerated) with clientStats carrying email/up/down/total/enable/expiryTime/reset/lastOnline (additive uuid/subId/inbound-level originNodeGuid/lastTrafficResetTime/trafficReset all ignored by the structs); /clients/get returns {client:{uuid,email,enable,flow,password,auth,expiryTime,totalGB,...},inboundIds} (PSP maps ID from uuid not id). Write path — a full client add->get->update(full-replace, totalGB 1GiB->2GiB reflected)->del round-trip plus bulkCreate ({created:1}) and bulkDel ({deleted:1}) all returned success:true, with post-del /clients/get answering ' (record not found)' (isClientNotFoundMsg hit). server/status (panelVersion 3.3.0), getPanelUpdateInfo ({currentVersion,latestVersion,updateAvailable}), getXrayVersion (array of tag strings), getWebCertFiles ({webCertFile,webKeyFile}) all intact. The 3.2.8->3.3.0 delta is purely ADDITIVE — new endpoints only (clients/groups/*, clients/bulkAdjust|bulkAttach|bulkDetach|delDepleted, custom-geo/*, nodes/*, xray/outbound-subs/*, server/getNew{UUID,VlessEnc,EchCert,mldsa65,mlkem768}) — none remove or change a route PSP touches, so min_xui stays 3.2.0 (MinXUI const/drift-guard untouched). Standalone-PSP caveat unchanged: 3X-UI's native multi-node (inbounds now carry originNodeGuid) stays a no-op because PSP's per-(panel,inbound)-unique email maps each client to exactly one inbound. NOTE: cookie (username/password) login was NOT re-verified on 3.3.0 — token-auth is PSP's verified/recommended path (3.2.x already noted cookie mode needs X-CSRF-Token for unsafe methods; token mode bypasses CSRF). 3.3.1 bump (2026-06-13) is LIVE-PANEL VERIFIED on a real 3.3.1 panel (xray 26.6.1) via API-token auth: all 17 non-destructive endpoints PSP calls round-tripped successfully and the two destructive ones (updatePanel, installXray) are present in the OpenAPI route table. Read path — server/status (panelVersion 3.3.1), getPanelUpdateInfo ({currentVersion:3.3.1,latestVersion:v3.3.1}), getXrayVersion (tag array), getWebCertFiles ({webCertFile,webKeyFile}), /inbounds/list + /inbounds/list/slim (settings/streamSettings/sniffing nested objects, clientStats carries id/inboundId/email/up/down/total/enable/expiryTime/reset/lastOnline; slim trims settings.clients to {email,enable}), /inbounds/get, /clients/get ({client:{uuid,...},inboundIds,usedTraffic} — usedTraffic is a new additive sibling key, dropped by Go; ID still mapped from client.uuid). Write path — inbounds add->get->update(read-modify-write preserving an existing client across a remark change)->setEnable->del all success:true; clients add->get->update(full-replace, totalGB 1GiB->2GiB reflected)->del (post-del get answers ' (record not found)', isClientNotFoundMsg hit), plus bulkCreate ({created:1}) and bulkDel ({deleted:1, new additive skipped[] ignored}); throwaway inbound cleaned up. The 3.3.0->3.3.1 delta (80 commits / 425 files) is purely ADDITIVE from PSP's view, confirmed against source at both tags: the bulk is a source-tree refactor (web/controller/* -> internal/web/controller/*, database/model/* -> internal/database/model/*, git renames 89-96%, invisible over HTTP); model.Client and xray.ClientTraffic are BYTE-IDENTICAL (tgId stays int64/number); model.Inbound only GAINED additive fields (SubSortIndex, ShareAddrStrategy, ShareAddr); the only response-shape change is the additive usedTraffic key on /clients/get; the only new inbound route is POST /inbounds/pushClientTraffics (multi-node, PSP never calls it); the service-layer 'match clients by email / preserve UUID on partial update' fixes align with how PSP already drives update/delete by email. Ships GHSA-jm48 (Xray log-path confinement) — a reason to adopt. No change to adapters/xui/client.go or types.go; min_xui stays 3.2.0 (MinXUI const/drift-guard untouched).
v3.6.0/v3.6.1 baseline — verified against 3X-UI 3.1.0 (client ops on the inbound-scoped endpoints). Do NOT run these PSP versions against 3X-UI 3.2.0; upgrade PSP to v3.6.2 first.
